name: Tomato Bean Polenta
recipeIngredient: 1 cup polenta or finely ground cornmeal
1 teaspoon ground basil or oregano (I used basil)
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring the water or stock to a boil in a large pot.
2. Slowly add the polenta, stirring constantly to avoid lumps.
3. Add the cumin and pepper. Reduce heat to low and cook, stirring often, for 20 minutes, until the polenta is thick and begins to pull away from the sides of the pot.
4. Stir in the basil and scallions. Set aside.
Sun-Dried Tomato Pesto:
1. Combine all pesto ingredients in a blender and process at high speed until well blended and smooth.
2. Transfer to a bowl and set aside.
Black Beans:
1. In a large saucepan or skillet, cook garlic, jalapeno, onion, pepper, and cumin in sherry for 3 minutes until soft.
2. Add the cooked black beans and continue to heat until hot.
3. If desired, add vegetable stock to achieve the desired moisture level.
Assembly:
1. Spoon polenta onto serving plates.
2. Garnish with tomato pesto.
3. Serve immediately or refrigerate leftovers.
